threat_description: Attacker spoofs a legitimate user identity/session to submit prompts and drive unintended agent actions.
mitigation: Strong authentication if applicable; signed/HttpOnly/SameSite cookies; CSRF protection; device fingerprinting and anomaly detection; rate limiting and CAPTCHA for public endpoints; short session lifetime and rotation.

threat_description: Users deny having issued prompts that caused actions or data access (lack of non-repudiation).
mitigation: End-to-end request logging with timestamps, user/session IDs, IPs; signed request IDs; immutable append-only logs; retain final answers, tool calls metadata and hashes to prove integrity.

threat_description: Agent connects to a spoofed or malicious LLM endpoint (DNS poisoning/MitM) leading to manipulated responses or API key theft.
mitigation: Enforce TLS 1.2+ with cert validation and optional certificate pinning; verify host allowlist; use official SDKs; validate response signatures if supported; restrict egress via firewall/DNS policies.

threat_description: Provider repudiates responses or usage; lack of traceability of prompts/responses for billing/security investigations.
mitigation: Store request/response transcripts and request IDs; enable provider-side logs; use signed receipts/webhooks where available; include deterministic request headers and nonces.

threat_description: Session hijack/CSRF leads the UI to accept actions as another user.
mitigation: Enable CSRF protection; strict session handling (HttpOnly, SameSite, secure); origin/referrer checks; OAuth/OpenID Connect for auth; rotate tokens; TLS everywhere.

threat_description: UI code or state is tampered via XSS/DOM injection or malicious Streamlit components, altering inputs sent to the agent.
mitigation: Escape/sanitize all untrusted content; avoid unsafe HTML; CSP with strict directives; limit third-party components; input validation; dependency pinning and SCA.

threat_description: Insufficient audit trails allow repudiation of UI submissions or displayed outputs.
mitigation: Structured, immutable logging of user actions, prompts, and result rendering events; correlate UI event IDs with agent/tool logs; time sync and log shipping.

threat_description: Sensitive info (API keys, system prompts, stack traces) displayed to end users through error banners or debug panels.
mitigation: Disable debug in prod; centralized error handling with redaction; never render secrets; redact PII; content security policy; segregate admin diagnostics from user UI.

threat_description: Flooding UI endpoints leads to resource exhaustion or excessive LLM invocations.
mitigation: Rate limiting, request quotas per IP/user; global concurrency caps; queue/backpressure; circuit breakers; caching; WAF/CDN.

threat_description: UI enables privilege escalation via unsafe execution hooks or excessive capabilities (e.g., executing arbitrary Python through components).
mitigation: Disable/avoid eval-like features; sandbox untrusted components; run with least OS privileges; code reviews for UI callbacks; dependency hardening.

threat_description: Spoofed tools or forged tool outputs mislead the agent (tool identity not authenticated).
mitigation: Whitelist tools by explicit, signed registration; verify tool caller identity; attach HMAC/signatures to tool results; bind tool schema to strict parsers.

threat_description: Prompt injection manipulates the agent to exfiltrate secrets, call dangerous tools, or alter chain logic.
mitigation: Segment user input from system/tool instructions; apply allowlists for tools; context filtering and output validators; instruction hierarchy enforcement; content/policy classifiers; limit tool arguments; use constrained decoding and schema validation.

threat_description: Lack of action traceability lets the agent repudiate tool calls or data access decisions.
mitigation: Comprehensive agent telemetry: log prompts, tool calls, parameters, observations, token counts; hash and timestamp each step; secure log storage; correlation IDs spanning UI→agent→tools.

threat_description: System prompt and secrets leakage via agent outputs or error traces.
mitigation: Never inject secrets into model context; secret redaction; response post-processing to block prompt/secret patterns; use tools for data retrieval instead of embedding secrets; disable verbose traces in prod; test with red-team prompts.

threat_description: Unbounded tool/LLM calling creates runaway costs and service degradation.
mitigation: Max-steps and token caps; timeouts; cost and call quotas per session/user; loop detection; circuit breakers; exponential backoff and global rate limits.

threat_description: Agent escalates privileges by invoking powerful local tools or code execution paths beyond intended scope.
mitigation: Run agent under least-privileged OS user; disable shell/code tools in production; sandbox (seccomp, containers); permission model for tool use; human-in-the-loop for sensitive actions.

threat_description: Unauthorized caller spoofs the agent to invoke tools directly.
mitigation: Internal auth for IPC calls (local tokens); process identity checks; restrict import paths; run dispatcher on loopback only; mTLS for inter-process HTTP if used.

threat_description: Tool parameters are tampered (e.g., SQL injection or path traversal) leading to unintended queries or file access.
mitigation: Strict input schemas and validation; parameterized SQL; denylist of dangerous SQL; allowlist tables/columns; sanitize paths; escape user-controlled values; use ORM with query builders.

threat_description: Lack of auditable logs allows repudiation of executed tools/queries.
mitigation: Structured logging of tool name, parameters, SQL text with parameter values, row counts, timing, caller identity; tamper-evident log storage.

threat_description: Sensitive DB data or file contents may be over-exposed to the agent beyond least privilege.
mitigation: Row/column-level filtering; data classification; output redaction; limit query breadth; PII masking; minimize tool return payloads; access controls per tool.

threat_description: Expensive or unbounded queries/tools cause resource exhaustion.
mitigation: Query timeouts and LIMITs; rate limits; concurrency controls; circuit breakers; pre-compute indexes; cache common results; cancel queries on session end.

threat_description: Dispatcher invokes OS commands, network, or file operations enabling privilege escalation/escape.
mitigation: Remove shelling-out; restrict OS capabilities (seccomp/apparmor); containerize tools; least-privilege filesystem access; egress firewall; code review for tool safety.

threat_description: DB file or contents are tampered (unauthorized writes/schema changes).
mitigation: File permissions (read-only where possible); run as non-root; mount DB read-only for read-mostly; SQLite integrity_check; backups with checksums; application-level integrity constraints.

threat_description: Lack of audit trail for data changes enables repudiation of modifications.
mitigation: Append-only audit table with triggers recording old/new values and actor; sign audit rows; time sync; protect logs.

threat_description: Unauthorized access to DB file discloses sensitive transactional data.
mitigation: Restrictive filesystem ACLs; encrypt DB at rest (SQLite SEE/SQLCipher); avoid storing secrets; secure backups; do not mount DB into UI container layers.

threat_description: DB locked or corrupted causing read failures and outages.
mitigation: Busy timeouts; read-only mode for readers; limit long transactions; periodic VACUUM/ANALYZE; health checks and automatic recovery from backups.

threat_description: Configuration/secrets tampering changes API keys or tool/DB endpoints.
mitigation: Immutable config in production; read-only mounts; signature/ checksum verification on startup; least-privileged file permissions; 4-eyes change control.

threat_description: No change history leads to repudiation of config modifications.
mitigation: Version control (private repo); signed commits; change tickets; checksum snapshots; store effective runtime config to an audit log on startup.

threat_description: Secrets/API keys disclosed via repo leaks, logs, or process dumps.
mitigation: Use a secrets manager; avoid committing .env; restrict read access; redact logs; rotate keys; avoid loading secrets into prompts; use env vars injected at runtime.

threat_description: Deleting or corrupting config prevents app/agent from starting or operating.
mitigation: Read-only mounts; startup validation with clear errors; fallback defaults for non-sensitive values; high-availability secret storage; backups and restore playbooks.

threat_description: Prompt tampering/injection to manipulate downstream agent and tools.
mitigation: Input validation and size caps; treat user input as untrusted context; strip/annotate instructions; use content filters; isolate user text from system prompts; disable markdown HTML where unsafe.

threat_description: User inadvertently submits sensitive data that could leak via logs or LLM context.
mitigation: Client-side warnings; server-side redaction (SSN, keys); prevent storing raw prompts; PII detection; selective context inclusion; per-tenant data boundaries.

threat_description: Flood of large prompts or file uploads causing resource exhaustion and LLM cost spikes.
mitigation: Request size limits; rate limiting; per-user quotas; early reject overly long inputs; streaming with backpressure; pre-check token estimates before forwarding to LLM.

threat_description: Response tampering (e.g., MitM, malicious extensions) injects scripts or alters results.
mitigation: TLS; CSP; Subresource Integrity for assets; disable inline scripts; no unsafe HTML rendering; escape model outputs; optional signed responses in zero-trust contexts.

threat_description: Outputs reveal system prompts, stack traces, or sensitive data.
mitigation: Output filters/redactors; safe error pages; block tokens matching secret patterns; evaluate prompts for leakage before rendering; role-based masking.

threat_description: Very large responses or streaming floods overwhelm the browser/client.
mitigation: Chunked streaming with limits; truncate long outputs; pagination; client timeouts; server-side response size caps.

threat_description: Submission payload is tampered (e.g., injected control tokens) altering agent behavior.
mitigation: Strict message schema; escape user content; separate channels for instructions vs data; sanitize/normalize inputs; use guardrails/policy models before agent consumption.

threat_description: Submission includes or exposes sensitive info that propagates into logs/LLM context.
mitigation: Minimize logging of inputs; redact secrets; PII detection; tenant isolation; encrypt in transit (loopback TLS if crossing hosts).

threat_description: Burst of submissions overwhelms agent pipelines and queues.
mitigation: Backpressure between UI and agent; per-session concurrency caps; request shedding; priority queues; circuit breakers.

element: LangChain ReAct Agent -> Streamlit UI (final answer)
threat_description: Tampered final answer (e.g., injected HTML/JS) before rendering.
mitigation: Sanitize/escape model outputs; disallow raw HTML rendering; content filters; neutralize links; safe Markdown rendering settings.

element: LangChain ReAct Agent -> Streamlit UI (final answer)
threat_description: Final answer includes sensitive data or prompts that should not be exposed.
mitigation: Apply post-answer redaction and policy checks; allowlist visible fields; mask PII; enforce tenant context constraints before display.

element: LangChain ReAct Agent -> Streamlit UI (final answer)
threat_description: Very large answers or infinite streams overwhelm UI pipeline.
mitigation: Response size/time caps; streaming with token budgets; cancelation support; truncation with user consent for expansion.

element: LangChain ReAct Agent -> Tool Dispatcher (tool call)
threat_description: Tampered tool call parameters (from compromised agent state) cause unintended operations.
mitigation: Strict tool schemas; parameter validation; allowlist of operations/resources; max rows and timeouts; require confirmation for sensitive tools (HITL).

element: LangChain ReAct Agent -> Tool Dispatcher (tool call)
threat_description: Sensitive data embedded in tool call arguments leaks to logs or other components.
mitigation: Avoid logging secrets; redact parameters; minimize argument payload; use in-memory passing; encrypt logs at rest with access controls.

element: LangChain ReAct Agent -> Tool Dispatcher (tool call)
threat_description: Excessive tool call volume causes queue saturation and resource depletion.
mitigation: Rate limit per session; concurrency pools; backpressure to agent; circuit breakers and retries with jitter; step budget enforcement.

threat_description: Tool results tampered or forged, misleading the agent.
mitigation: Sign tool outputs; integrity checks (hash of query + parameters + rows); transmit over authenticated channel; verify schema/shape upon receipt.

threat_description: Observations reveal more data than necessary (over-broad rows).
mitigation: Apply LIMIT and column selection; mask PII; partition/tenant filters; redact before returning; enforce max payload bytes.

threat_description: Large result sets overwhelm the agent's memory or token budgets.
mitigation: Row caps and pagination; sample/aggregate; early truncation; stream incrementally with stop conditions; enforce token budget checks.

threat_description: Query text tampered (dynamic string concatenation) enabling unintended writes or data exposure.
mitigation: Parameterized queries only; prepared statements; avoid string building; static query templates; least privilege (read-only) connections.

threat_description: Query parameters include sensitive data that may be logged by SQLite or OS.
mitigation: Disable SQL logging in prod; scrub parameters before logs; secure DB temporary files; use in-memory parameters; encrypt swap/disable core dumps.

threat_description: Expensive queries (full scans, no indexes) starve DB, causing timeouts.
mitigation: Indexes; LIMIT + WHERE; query planner hints; timeout settings; rate limits; reject long-running queries; analyze and optimize queries.

threat_description: Rows tampered (corrupt DB/file) causing incorrect observations.
mitigation: DB integrity_check; checksums; verify row counts/types; fail closed on corruption; restore from trusted backups.

threat_description: Returned rows include sensitive fields not intended for the agent.
mitigation: Projection of explicit columns; view-based access; data masking; confidential attributes filtered in SQL layer.

threat_description: Huge result sets cause memory/processing exhaustion.
mitigation: LIMIT; pagination/streaming; cap payload bytes; cancel queries exceeding thresholds; backpressure to caller.

element: Config files (.env, yaml, toml) -> LangChain ReAct Agent (Agent config & API key)
threat_description: Config values tampered in transit/load (e.g., overridden by env) altering agent behavior.
mitigation: Load-order hardening; only read from trusted paths; verify checksums/signatures; lock down environment variable injection; assert schema and ranges for config values.

element: Config files (.env, yaml, toml) -> LangChain ReAct Agent (Agent config & API key)
threat_description: API key exposure during load (logs, exceptions, memory dumps).
mitigation: Avoid logging secrets; mask on print; secure memory handling; disable core dumps; role-based access to env/config; rotate keys on incident.

element: Config files (.env, yaml, toml) -> LangChain ReAct Agent (Agent config & API key)
threat_description: Missing/invalid config or key leads to agent failures and outages.
mitigation: Startup validation with clear errors; fallback to degraded mode; health checks; secret manager with HA; retries with jitter.

element: Config files (.env, yaml, toml) -> Tool Dispatcher (Tool/DB config)
threat_description: DB path/params tampered to point to malicious or non-existent DB.
mitigation: Validate config against allowlists; canonicalize and verify DB location; require file ownership/permissions; refuse network paths if not needed.

element: Config files (.env, yaml, toml) -> Tool Dispatcher (Tool/DB config)
threat_description: Sensitive config (paths, credentials) gets exposed via logs or errors.
mitigation: Do not log full config; mask sensitive fields; minimal error messages; separate secrets from non-secrets; limit read access.

element: Config files (.env, yaml, toml) -> Tool Dispatcher (Tool/DB config)
threat_description: Invalid/slow DB configs cause dispatcher unavailability.
mitigation: Validate DB connectivity at startup; timeouts and retries; fall back to read-only or cached mode; circuit breaker for DB-dependent tools.

element: LangChain ReAct Agent -> LLM Provider (OpenAI/HF/Ollama) (API key / LLM request)
threat_description: Request tampering or MitM discloses API key or alters prompt.
mitigation: TLS with cert validation/pinning; HTTPS-only; minimize key scope; rotate keys; avoid sending secrets in prompts; sign requests if supported.

element: LangChain ReAct Agent -> LLM Provider (OpenAI/HF/Ollama) (API key / LLM request)
threat_description: Prompts unintentionally exfiltrate confidential data to provider or outside tenant boundary.
mitigation: Do not include secrets in prompts; provider data control settings; selective redaction; enterprise tenancy with data retention off; PHI/PII filters.

element: LangChain ReAct Agent -> LLM Provider (OpenAI/HF/Ollama) (API key / LLM request)
threat_description: Excessive/automated requests drive cost spikes and rate-limit/DoS.
mitigation: Per-user/session budgets; global quotas; adaptive rate limiting; batching; caching; preflight token estimation; hard cost ceilings.

element: LLM Provider (OpenAI/HF/Ollama) -> LangChain ReAct Agent (LLM response)
threat_description: Response tampering or compromised provider returns manipulated or adversarial outputs.
mitigation: Validate outputs against schemas; toxicity/safety filters; cross-check critical answers; deterministic temperature for sensitive tasks; provider trust and SLAs.

element: LLM Provider (OpenAI/HF/Ollama) -> LangChain ReAct Agent (LLM response)
threat_description: Responses leak other tenants’ data or echo back confidential prompts.
mitigation: Enterprise isolation features; no-training/retention flags; contractual DP/segregation assurances; post-response redaction; anomaly detection for out-of-scope entities.

element: LLM Provider (OpenAI/HF/Ollama) -> LangChain ReAct Agent (LLM response)
threat_description: Very long or looping responses consume resources and block pipelines.
mitigation: Set max tokens and timeouts; stop sequences; streaming with early cutoff; cancelation support; limit retries on 429/5xx.
